title: Outcome Learning and Skill Promotion
summary: Evaluate completed runs, classify success/failure, extract learning proposals, compare old/new behavior, and promote or roll back skills only after evidence and human approval.
original_problem: RTS records outcomes and contains legacy evolution/success specifications, but current execution results do not yet flow into governed, tested, versioned skill improvement.
why_it_matters: Without this child the system can execute but cannot reliably improve its methods or prevent repeated mistakes.
reason_frozen: Requires completed asset, read-only, and execution-controller children plus real run evidence for evaluation design.
preserved_value: Outcome records, learning proposals, regression comparison, skill lifecycle, approval, rollback, retirement, and reconstruction.
trigger_conditions: At least three governed run outcomes exist
Skill promotion and rollback datasets are defined
negative_triggers: Success is accepted without linked evidence
Core rules can be silently rewritten
source_refs: RTS-Talent-Registry promotion asset via asset manifest
